'R2R3-MYB' is not explicitly written in the provided text, but MYB TF plus EAR motif is supported; overall phrasing is acceptable though subtype is not directly stated.

Regulator_Type.reason: Explicitly described as an MYB transcription factor.

Source_Species.reason: Maize is the study species for ZmMYB127.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Maize is identified as Zea mays L.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Knockout mutants were generated in the B73 inbred line.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Applied species is maize, Zea mays.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: B73 used for knockout and analyses; B104 used for transformation of overexpression construct; OE introgressed into Zheng58 and Chang7-2 to generate Zhengdan958.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results directly measured starch, protein, vitamins B6/B9, and P/Fe/Zn in kernels and showed ZmMYB127 affects them.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: These products correspond to polysaccharide, protein, vitamins, and mineral nutrients, all supported by measured traits.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Paper states CSE and AL are main storage sites and concludes ZmMYB127 regulates nutrient deposition in both AL and CSE/endosperm.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Reserve accumulation and grain nutritional quality are central measured outputs in Results.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Knockout reduced endosperm filling, AL maintenance, starch/protein, vitamins B6/B9, P/Fe/Zn, and kernel weight/hardness/test weight; OE improved these.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: Results show ZmMYB127 is a filling-endosperm-specific dual-function regulator, and targeted overexpression improved yield/quality traits without obvious agronomic penalties.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods appear in Results/Methods for ZmMYB127: CRISPR, OE, RT-qPCR, CUT&Tag-seq, EMSA, DLR, pull-down, BiFC, Co-IP, field trials, nutrient profiling, cytology.

Regulation_Mode.reason: Directly shown as both transcriptional activator and repressor.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: Results show activation of NKD1/2 and repression of O2/CR4; AL9, zein genes and starch-related genes are also affected.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: NKD1, NKD2, O2 and CR4 are the four highlighted direct targets with promoter binding/functional assays.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: Supported by phenotypes and transcriptomics: endosperm filling, reserve biosynthesis, AL development/maintenance, nutrient deposition.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: O2 binding to ZmMYB127 promoter and transactivation of ZmMYB127 are directly shown.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: Results support activation complex with O2 and repressive ternary complex with ZmLUG3 and ZmABI4.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Physical interaction with O2 and ZmLUG3 is directly shown.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results state endosperm-specific strongest expression during filling stage (10–36 DAP) and ubiquitous distribution across endosperm regions.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: OE under 27-kDa γ-zein promoter in filling endosperm is directly described.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: BiFC with O2 restored strong nuclear YFP signal for ZmMYB127/O2 complex; repression/activation assays are nuclear TF-consistent. While standalone localization is not directly stated, nucleus is directly implicated.

Applied_Promoters.reason: 27-kDa γ-zein promoter explicitly used for ZmMYB127 overexpression and FLAG construct.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Field trials across years/locations Hefei, Sanya, Zhanjiang are explicitly described.

Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation.reason: Results show O2 activates ZmMYB127 and ZmMYB127 represses O2, establishing reciprocal circuitry.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Overexpression lines showed no significant differences in plant height, ear height, or kernel number per ear; hybrid also lacked obvious agronomic penalties.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Overexpression in inbred and elite hybrid improved traits; Discussion explicitly suggests precision breeding, promoter/CRE editing, and EAR motif editing strategies.

Omics_Data.reason: Data availability lists PRJCA044247/CRA028659 for CUT&Tag-seq and PRJCA047065/CRA030621 for RNA-seq.
